text: So we have a young patient who has a second surgery for endometriosis in two months. The first one was done one month ago. I performed colorectal resection for occlusive endometriosis. And she also presents severe endometriosis. endometriosis of the diaphragm and I scheduled the second surgery in lateral decubitus position in order to perform excision of this huge endometriosis lesions.

So after the first surgery, the patient feels very, very well. And she was very courageous to have the second surgery, but she has normal bowel movements and actually no pain. One month after the first surgery, she was very motivated to come back and to to treat these lesions which are responsible for severe chest and right shoulder pain.
